arts.ttl: Serie A | Napoli v Roma: Probable starting lineups
arts.sum: Roma face injury concerns ahead of their crucial match with Napoli, with star forward Paulo Dybala likely limited to a substitute role due to ongoing muscular issues. New manager Claudio Ranieri is taking a cautious approach with the Argentine playmaker, whose absence significantly impacts the team’s performance, reducing their win rate by 13%. Despite these setbacks, Roma’s top scorer Artem Dovbyk is expected to start, though he’s carrying a minor knee problem. The team’s attacking options are further limited with Eldor Shomurodov sidelined, while Spanish defender Mario Hermoso is also unavailable due to injury. arts.ttl: Ranieri’s Roma away to league leaders Napoli
arts.sum: Roma were unbeaten in their two league matches against Napoli last season (2-0 at the Olimpico stadium in December 2023 and 2-2 at the Maradona stadium in April 2024), the Giallorossi could register their longest streak without defeat against the Partenopei in Serie A for the first time since the period between 2015 and 2016 (four matches in that case). Roma have won only three of their 12 games in the current league season, registering five defeats (D4): since the beginning of the 1980s, the Giallorossi have always obtained at least four wins in their first 13 games played in Serie A, and only twice have they lost six matches (in 2011/12 and 2008/09).
